versionNotes: Phase 1 uses a statewide Florida snapshot grounded in current official pages and support-center articles.
workflowNotes: Create an account and complete the prioritization questionnaire before the inspection application.
The free wind-mitigation inspection and the grant application stay separate.
The initial report should surface recommended improvements and cost estimates before the project-choice step.
Only recommended or later observed improvements should move into the grant workflow.
keyGrantRules: The grant may reimburse up to 2-to-1 matching funds up to $10,000 under the current public rules.
Low-income households may qualify for up to $10,000 without a homeowner match.
Work started before grant approval may be denied.
The grant application window can close 24 months after the initial inspection.
prioritizationNotes: Group and portal timing can affect when a homeowner can proceed.
Support-state routes exist, but they stay behind the core decision wedge in phase 1.
contractorResponsibilityNotes: The homeowner is responsible for choosing, documenting, and managing the contractor.
The program no longer maintains an authorized contractor list for homeowners to rely on.
Permit responsibility still needs to be handled and closed out before final inspection.
attachedHomeScopeNotes: Attached single-family homes can be treated as townhouses for grant scope.
Townhouse treatment can narrow eligible grant work to opening protection.
